name: Anne Murray
abstract: Morna Anne Murray (born June 20, 1945) is a Canadian retired country, pop and adult contemporary music singer who has sold over 55 million album copies worldwide during her over 40-year career. Murray has won four Grammys including the Grammy Award for Best Female Pop Vocal Performance in 1978.

about_quotes.speaker: John Budd
about_quotes.evidence: I met her one time just by asking where she was in Las Vegas and her manager said to come and see the show and come downstairs in the dressing room later.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Budd interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997 [transcript only]

about_quotes.speaker: Kristen Korb
about_quotes.evidence: I thought was going to be Barbara Mandrell. I loved playing guitar, I did Anne Murray songs... I'm sure there's a good bit of Barbara Mandrell and Anne Murray and Tom T. Hall inside of me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kristen Korb interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 16, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Peter Appleyard
about_quotes.evidence: I recorded with Anne Murray, Gordon Lightfoot, oh gosh, I can’t remember them all.
about_quotes.doc_title: Peter Appleyard interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, October 4, 2012
